lofi.notes: Minor color blends with mellow beats.

downtempo.notes: Strong: mellow melodic lines sit well over laid-back organic drums (especially half-time feels).

electronic.notes: Fairly workable because the mode is familiar; keep phrasing raga-like to avoid generic minor EDM.

reggae_dub.notes: Strong: minor dub fits well, but keep phrase endings and rests consistent so it doesn't drift into other minor ragas.

trap.notes: Strong: minor hooks translate well; keep motifs rooted in the raga's characteristic contour rather than generic minor scales.

lofi.notes: Soft, intimate mood works well with mellow lofi textures and gentle beats.

ambient.notes: Sustained Ma and komal Ga lend themselves to meditative soundscapes.

calm.notes: Ideal for late-night calm, deep focus, and reflective content.

upbeat.notes: The raga's soft late-night character does not naturally push energy.

jazz_fusion.notes: Dorian-like with sweet komal Ga/Ma for intimate modal play.

cinematic.notes: Excellent for intimate melancholy and 'night-romance' cues; works beautifully with soft strings and woodwinds.

downtempo.notes: Intimate romantic flow in organic drums.

electronic.notes: Melodic phrases adapt but keep mid-tempo.

chillhop.notes: Sweet melancholy ideal for warm jazzy beats.

trap.notes: Intimate lines can serve subtle hooks.

rock.notes: Soft character better in ballad rock.

lofi.notes: The augmented seconds add exotic colour to lofi. Works best with slower, more contemplative beats. The gravity may clash with overly bright lofi textures.

ambient.notes: Bhairav's solemn mood translates powerfully to ambient. The komal Re and komal Dha sustained over drones create deeply evocative atmospheres.

calm.notes: Bhairav is more intense than 'calm' — but at slow tempos with careful phrasing, it achieves a profound stillness.

upbeat.notes: The serious, majestic character doesn't naturally lend itself to upbeat production, though Bhairav-based trance or psytrance can be powerful.

jazz_fusion.notes: Double-harmonic exotic tension for adventurous modal jazz.

cinematic.notes: Excellent for solemn, devotional, and mythic grandeur; augmented-second flavor is immediately dramatic.

electronic.notes: Augmented seconds add edge to synths at moderate BPM.

chillhop.notes: Solemn lines add depth to chill grooves.

trap.notes: Exotic tension for dark trap hooks.

electronic.note: oscillations need time; atmospheric lead over minimalist beats

lofi.notes: The minor/Phrygian tonality creates a naturally melancholic, introspective mood perfect for lofi. Komal Ga and komal Ni add emotional depth over mellow beats.

ambient.notes: Bhairavi is arguably the most ambient-compatible raga. Its devotional stillness and komal palette create deeply meditative soundscapes.

calm.notes: The gentle, komal-heavy scale naturally produces calm textures. Works beautifully at slow tempos with sustained notes.

upbeat.notes: Less natural fit for upbeat styles due to the minor tonality, but Bhairavi thumris can be lively. Requires careful arrangement.

jazz_fusion.notes: Phrygian-inflected scale offers exotic tension for modal solos; melancholic compassion fits bluesy feels.

cinematic.notes: Deep emotional pathos ideal for dramatic scoring and closers.

downtempo.notes: Versatile komal palette creates intimate organic grooves.

electronic.notes: Proven in electronic disco; all-komal phrases adapt to synth arps.

chillhop.notes: Melancholic lines pair with walking bass warmth.

reggae_dub.notes: Compassionate mood suits dub delay and deep bass.

trap.notes: Can translate into dark hooks; best as short, memorable motifs with 808 glides echoing raga bends.

rock.notes: Phrygian/minor-rock language is familiar; use bends and slides to preserve the raga character.

lofi.notes: Pentatonic clarity blends well with mellow beats and simple harmonic beds.

ambient.notes: Open intervals and lack of half-steps make Bhoopali ideal for ambient textures.

calm.notes: Clean melodic lines suit meditation, yoga, and quiet focus.

upbeat.notes: Pentatonic structure can support upbeat rhythms if phrasing is kept crisp.

jazz_fusion.notes: Major pentatonic simplicity allows open jazz improvisation.

cinematic.notes: Bright serenity for hopeful or pastoral scenes.

downtempo.notes: Excellent: pentatonic motifs loop cleanly and stay singable over organic drums.

electronic.notes: Very strong: pentatonic hooks survive synth treatment and quantization, making it ideal for arps and toplines.

chillhop.notes: Bright clean melodies perfect with vinyl warmth.

reggae_dub.notes: Good fit: offbeat skanks + pentatonic lead feels natural; keep the bass on stable tones.

trap.notes: Excellent for catchy hooks; pentatonic contour reads instantly even over dense drums.

rock.notes: Excellent: maps well to guitar and riff logic; easy to keep melodic identity even at higher tempos.

lofi.notes: Warm major scale works well for mellow beats.

ambient.notes: Stable tonal center supports long pads.

jazz_fusion.notes: Plain major scale for straightforward major-key jazz.

cinematic.notes: Bright stable for uplifting or heroic themes.

downtempo.notes: Good: major-mode warmth works well at 80–100 BPM, especially with acoustic instrumentation.

electronic.notes: Strong: major hooks/arps work immediately; keep melodic grammar raga-like so it doesn't become plain EDM-pop.

reggae_dub.notes: Good: major reggae is common; anchor bass on stable tones and keep the lead lightly ornamented.

rock.notes: Strong: major-scale riffs and melodies map well to guitars; can support energetic rock without losing coherence.

lofi.notes: Slow, weighty phrases can add gravitas to late-night lofi.

ambient.notes: Sustained oscillations and deep mood align strongly with ambient textures.

calm.notes: More solemn than calm; works when calm is interpreted as deep stillness.

upbeat.notes: The raga's heavy gravity does not naturally support upbeat energy.

jazz_fusion.notes: Natural-minor feel with andolan suits slow blues/jazz, but gravity limits fast improv.

cinematic.notes: Majestic somber depth perfect for epic orchestral tension.

downtempo.notes: Patient exploration thrives in spacious organic mixes.

electronic.notes: Heavy andolan loses character above 100 BPM; better at slower house.

reggae_dub.notes: Low-register gravity anchors deep dub bass perfectly.

trap.notes: Grave mood clashes with aggressive hi-hats; too patient for rolls.

rock.notes: Majestic power works in slow heavy riffs, not fast punk.

lofi.notes: Mixolydian warmth + romantic mood = perfect lofi match. The komal Ni adds a blues tinge that pairs naturally with hip-hop influenced beats.

ambient.notes: Desh can work in ambient but its playful character prefers more rhythmic settings. Best for brighter, more uplifting ambient.

calm.notes: Naturally calm and warm. The Mixolydian brightness prevents heaviness while the komal Ni adds emotional depth. Perfect for relaxation.

upbeat.notes: Desh is the most naturally upbeat of the common ragas. Its bright major-ish scale and playful phrasing suit higher-energy production well.

jazz_fusion.notes: Mixolydian warmth with komal Ni ideal for funky modal jazz.

cinematic.notes: Playful romantic for lighter narrative scoring.

downtempo.notes: Excellent: phrases feel natural at 80–100 BPM and retain sweetness even with modern drum kits.

electronic.notes: Very strong: melodic hooks survive synth layers and sidechain pumping; keep the raga's characteristic turns so it doesn't become generic pop.

chillhop.notes: Excellent: sits right on top of swing drums and jazzy bass; the melodic contour feels inherently 'song-like'.

reggae_dub.notes: Strong: Mixolydian family fits reggae harmonic language; works well for offbeat skanks + melodic answering phrases.

trap.notes: Works well as a hook raga; keep motifs tight and let 808 slides complement the melodic bends.

rock.notes: Strong: blends with major/DOM rock harmony; riffs can carry the raga if you keep the specific melodic grammar.

lofi.notes: Soft minor color suits relaxed beats.

ambient.notes: Gentle phrasing works with long pads.

jazz_fusion.notes: Dorian-like core makes it one of the easiest ragas to blend with jazz harmony; great for modal vamps and extended chords.

chillhop.notes: Top-tier: chillhop already lives in Dorian/ii-minor territory; walking bass + swing drums pair extremely well.

downtempo.notes: Excellent: sits naturally on earthy grooves and can handle long, meditative repetition.

electronic.notes: Good: Dorian hooks survive synth treatment; keep melodic ornaments audible so it doesn't become generic minor.

reggae_dub.notes: Strong: minor/Dorian bass motion is common in dub; keep melodic phrases simple and let delay do the motion.

trap.notes: Good for darker hooks; keep motifs short and use subtle micro-bends rather than long taans.

rock.notes: Good for jam-band/prog rock; Dorian is familiar on guitar and can sustain long solos.

lofi.notes: Warm and romantic character suits mellow beats.

ambient.notes: Soft tension from komal Ni works well in spacious textures.

chillhop.notes: Excellent: fits swing and walking bass; use jazzy chords but keep them slow-moving.

downtempo.notes: Excellent: naturally grooves at mid-tempo, and semi-classical ornamentation reads clearly over sparse drums.

electronic.notes: Very strong: makes catchy toplines; keep the characteristic descending color so it doesn't collapse into plain pop.

reggae_dub.notes: Strong: dominant-mode language fits reggae; skanks + melodic answers feel natural.

trap.notes: Good: makes memorable hooks; keep the motif compact so it survives busy hats and 808 movement.

rock.notes: Good: easy to map onto guitar and common rock harmony, especially in jam/prog contexts.

lofi.notes: Minor pentatonic is the backbone of hip-hop melody. Malkauns maps perfectly to lofi's introspective mood. The absence of Pa creates a haunting, unresolved quality ideal for late-night beats.

ambient.notes: Perhaps the single most ambient-compatible raga. The pentatonic spaciousness, the meditative mood, the deep-night association — all align perfectly with ambient aesthetics.

calm.notes: Deeply calm, but with an intensity that goes beyond mere relaxation. Works for meditation and deep focus tracks.

upbeat.notes: Malkauns is fundamentally a raga of stillness and depth. Upbeat treatment would fight against its nature.

jazz_fusion.notes: Pentatonic minor variant offers spacious modal freedom.

cinematic.notes: Haunting meditative quality for profound scenes.

downtempo.notes: Wide note gaps create natural spaciousness.

electronic.notes: Proven in disco; pentatonic phrases loop cleanly on synths.

chillhop.notes: Meditative lines pair beautifully with jazzy swing.

reggae_dub.notes: Strong fit: minor pentatonic lines sit naturally over heavy sub-bass; dub delay complements the spacious phrasing.

trap.notes: Great for catchy minor hooks; pentatonic contour makes motifs easy to remember and replay.

rock.notes: Works well for riff-based rock/psych; pentatonic shapes map naturally onto guitar.

cinematic.notes: Outstanding for dusk-time suspense and existential tension; the lack of easy resolution is a scoring superpower.

electronic.notes: Dissonance adds edge but needs careful pacing.

jazz_fusion.notes: Lydian b2 dissonance for tense modal jazz.

electronic.note: needs silence and micro-tension; better as intro textures

cinematic.notes: Top-tier for mystery + devotional tension; handles orchestral pads, ominous swells, and poignant solo statements very well.

jazz_fusion.notes: Complex Lydian b2 b6 chromaticism for advanced modal work.

ambient.notes: Slow phrases work well for meditative textures.

cinematic.notes: One of the strongest for devotional intensity, sorrow, and high drama; the scale profile creates built-in tension.

lofi.notes: Works best with sparse, minimal beats.

jazz_fusion.notes: Exotic Phrygian#4 for adventurous modal work.

electronic.notes: Proven in electronic; tivra Ma adds color.

lofi.notes: Lydian brightness pairs beautifully with lofi warmth. The major 7th (Ni) and #4 (tivra Ma) create dreamy, nostalgic textures. Use slow Ga-centered melodies over mellow beats.

jazz.notes: Lydian is native to jazz harmony (Lydian Chromatic Concept). Maps directly to maj7#11 chords. Works over Cmaj7#11, Dmaj7, and related voicings.

ambient.notes: The serene, devotional mood translates perfectly to ambient textures. Sustained Ga and Ni over drone-like pads create meditative soundscapes.

classical_fusion.notes: Strong melodic structure adapts well to orchestral or chamber arrangements. The clear aroha/avaroha gives composers a framework to build on.

electronic.notes: Strong for arps and toplines; the bright #4 color stays distinctive even with synth sound design if you avoid busy chord-changes.

r_and_b.notes: The major 7th and romantic mood overlap with R&B harmonic sensibilities. The #4 adds an exotic colour to standard progressions.

rock.notes: Bright energy translates to guitar leads with drive.

hip_hop.notes: Yaman melodic fragments work as sample material or melodic hooks. The devotional mood adds depth to introspective tracks.

jazz_fusion.notes: Lydian mode with sharp Ma aligns perfectly with modal jazz; serene romantic mood supports cool improvisation and extended harmonies.

cinematic.notes: Naturally uplifting and devotional, with a clear 'arrival' note feel that supports wide pads, strings, and gentle climaxes.

downtempo.notes: Sits comfortably over organic 80–100 BPM grooves; phrases can loop without losing identity if you keep a drone / pedal tone.

chillhop.notes: Melodic warmth pairs naturally with jazzy drums and vinyl crackle.

reggae_dub.notes: Romantic lift works with offbeat skanks, though higher register needs deep bass anchoring.

trap.notes: Uplifting phrases make strong melodic hooks over 808s.
